title: Dino Gas: A Prehistoric Climate Changer?
story: Dinosaurs might have been nature's original greenhouse gas producers! In 2012, scientists made a surprising discovery about our prehistoric pals. Their research suggested that dinosaur flatulence - yes, dino toots - may have actually warmed the Earth. These giant creatures produced so much gas that it could have affected the planet's climate. It's a stinky but fascinating idea that shows how even the smallest actions can have big impacts on our world.
activity_question: What did scientists suggest dinosaur flatulence might have done?
follow_up_question: Can you think of any modern animals that might produce a lot of gas like dinosaurs did?

title: MTV Breaks Barriers with Gender-Neutral Awards
story: Lights, camera, action! MTV made history on May 7, 2017, by becoming the first major awards show to introduce gender-neutral categories. Instead of separate awards for actors and actresses, MTV celebrated talent regardless of gender. Emma Watson took home the Best Film Actor award, while Millie Bobby Brown claimed the Best TV Actor prize. This groundbreaking move sparked conversations about inclusivity in the entertainment world.

title: Beethoven's Masterpiece Debuts
story: The audience held their breath as the first notes rang out. Ludwig van Beethoven's 9th Symphony was about to change music forever. On May 7, 1824, this groundbreaking piece premiered in Vienna, Austria. The Theater am Kärntnertor buzzed with excitement. Beethoven's latest work wasn't just a symphony—it boldly included singers, a first for its time. Despite being almost completely deaf, the composer had created a masterpiece that would inspire generations.
activity_question: Where did Beethoven's 9th Symphony premiere?
bonus_fact: Beethoven's 9th Symphony is also known as the Choral Symphony because it features singers.

story: Buried secrets came to light when archaeologist Ehud Netzer made a thrilling discovery in 2007. After years of searching, he found the long-lost tomb of King Herod the Great at Herodium in the West Bank. This May 7 revelation solved a mystery that had puzzled historians for two millennia, offering new insights into the famous ancient ruler's life and death.
activity_question: Where was King Herod's tomb discovered?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it took so long to find King Herod's tomb?

title: William Penn's Bold Stand for Freedom
story: William Penn took a brave step for equality. On May 7, 1700, he started monthly meetings for Black people to discuss ending slavery. Penn, who founded Pennsylvania, believed everyone deserved freedom. These gatherings gave people a chance to speak up and work towards a fairer world.
activity_question: What did William Penn start on May 7, 1700?
follow_up_question: Why do you think these meetings were important?

title: Breaking Barriers in the Virgin Islands
story: History was made in 1946 when William H. Hastie took office as governor of the U.S. Virgin Islands. On May 7, he became the first Black person to hold this important position. Hastie's appointment marked a significant step forward in the fight for equal representation in government leadership roles.
activity_question: What position did William H. Hastie take in 1946?
activity_choices: Governor of the U.S. Virgin Islands
activity_answer: Governor of the U.S. Virgin Islands
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important for people of all backgrounds to have a chance to be leaders?

title: A Day for Moms Becomes Official
story: Flowers, cards, and breakfast in bed! In 1914, the US Congress made Mother's Day an official holiday. On May 7 that year, they passed a resolution to honor moms across the country. Now, every second Sunday in May, families celebrate the amazing women who care for them.
activity_question: What did the US Congress do in 1914?
activity_choices: Made Mother's Day an official holiday
Created a new type of flower
activity_answer: Made Mother's Day an official holiday
follow_up_question: How do you celebrate Mother's Day in your family?
